div1.entryFree: Hew (impf. not used, partic. --ed: Tit. II, 4, 17. --n: H6C II, 2, 168. V, 4, 69), to cut, to chop, to hack: H6A IV, 7, 47. H6C II, 6, 48. Cor. IV, 5, 126. Tit. I, 97. Tit. I, 97 II, 4, 17. Caes. II, 1, 174. to h. down (a tree): H6C II, 1, 55. II, 2, 168. Cor. I, 1, 185. Mcb. V, 4, 4. h. up, in the same sense: I could h. up rocks, H6B V, 1, 24. must by the roots be --n up, H6C V, 4, 69. to h. out == to make by cutting: or h. my way out with a bloody axe, H6C III, 2, 181. to h. to == to shape by cutting (cf. To): thou rather shalt enforce it with thy smile than h. to't with thy sword, Tim. V, 4, 46 (O. Edd. too't). In H5 II, 1, 39 O. Edd. unintelligibly hewn, M. Edd. drawn.
